item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

Image is derived from here, which is under public domain. As such, this image is CC 3.0 compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you open the collapsible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*reads post *
me: meh. what do they mean "write in blood"
*sees image *
me: WHAT!!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that what you type gets written in killings is not explained enough, I would say that it isn't explained at all. I had to read the comments to get it, and that's too bad. Downvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might not be getting this because I am too tired while I was reading this, but in summary you seem to be saying the message input into the machine is spelled out on the map…
If not, were the content of the message meant to make a difference in the actual result?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Confession time: I really did expect the 'message' to be a drawing of a penis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the article. Need a better map, but I'm the only one who would give a shit about it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the description and loved the picture. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It appears 46 only fits into Europe and China's systems. (Interestingly, from what I read, Modopoint seems to be the closest thing to SI standard…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing I completely forgot to explicitly mention in my article (perhaps for the better) is that the locations of the murders, when connected, are supposed to spell out the message typed on 1749-1. Whoops…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was bugging me too. I'm going to assume that you meant the European sizing system, because reading it as a US shoe size is ludicrous- at 6'6" with size 46 feet these guys would either have to be gigantic hobbits or mass produced extra-wide Incredible Hulks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new image is way too big. You really don't need 3.5 Mb to display a 300 pixel pic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this is what it looks like without the lines. I think a solid case for "LOOK WHAT WE FOUND" can be made using context clues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was waffling, and then I got to the map… and, well, I guess my issue is, it only makes a message if you are specifically looking for that message. You could use red lines to turn it into a picture of a man, if you wanted to…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The arrows in the logo are pointing the wrong direction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha ha ha… you're never going to guess what the last line in the dialogue almost was ha ha ha…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it in the forums, and I still like it here. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie, I was terrified that the last line from the hitman was going to involve AWCY, and then I would have to cyanide myself. As it stands, the article is quite interesting indeed, and the final picture adds a nice bit of punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the map. I also liked the interview. We seem to be on an "artificially created humans" kick right now though.

Also, that guy's feet are more than four times the size of mine, and I'm considered to have large feet. I think you're missing something there. (EDIT: Herp, Sensei got it right.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
black dress shoes, size 46.

Under which system? Different regions have different measures for shoes. My shoes show(ed) sizes for US, Europe, Mexico, UK, and Japan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Upon the activation and input of a message into SCP-1749-1, numerous instances of SCP-1749-1 will materialize in populated locations around the continental United States."

I think you made a mistake here - wasn't it supposed to be 1749-2? In fact this sentence seems redundant with what was written two paragraphs earlier.

Okay, let me get this straight. The Foundation activated an SCP that murders people (not just D-class, civilians) in locations you cannot foresee outright just so they'd have a 1% chance of the cyanide pill failing and procuring a 1749-2 example for interrogation? I find that hard to believe and the resulting interview is unnecessary anyway. I'd rather it was simply pointed in the article that the map of murders spells a message you've input.

The Beta incident is good though. Perfect way to end the article on a chilling note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It might be a drone, but it seems to be obtained from a previously existing person. A copy?

Upvoted, even if I feel the entire article grows around a device that makes literal the sentence "we only want to send them a message."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good in many ways, but there's a few problems I have with it. First off, these sentences in the third paragraph of the description are more or less redundant with earlier information in it (also, the second -1 should be -2).

Upon the activation and input of a message into SCP-1749-1, numerous instances of SCP-1749-1 will materialize in populated locations around the continental United States. No more than one instance has been observed in any single location.

Secondly, I don't like how rude the -1 instance is. It's a drone, with the purpose of sending a message. I feel it shouldn't really care enough to bother insulting the doctor. Thirdly, why those lines connected on the map? That's obviously not the only way to connect the dots on the murders, so why choose those without explanation? Either say "connected in an approximation of the message", which gives away too much, or leave the lines off, but perhaps add a few more dots to make it clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not quite sure what's going on, and if someone could explain in a collapsible I'd be appreciative. That said, it was good and I liked it. I wasn't sure how I was going to vote until I hit the map and went, "crap, what did they find?" and then I had to upvote. Props for creativity man. Bravo! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love this as you've written it, especially how Eskobar described it. It gets me excited to read it. I get it man, I get it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love, love, love the content of the message that was sent. If only they'd just typed "Test"! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this is… really different. And cool. One thing I have to say is the dialogue is a bit on the nose, but other than that you did an excellent job. And better to be on the nose than to have some people miss the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…um. Wow. It really seems like such a blue and orange morality situation, where human lives are being ended literally because they helped to form a murder message. I've actually had thoughts before that something like this could be an effective method for executing terrorist attacks. I give automatic points for creativity, even when I don't upvote. You get those points.

Unrelated note: I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it, though the picture I do wish was a bit… better? More detailed? Does seem a little MS paint now that you mention it… Maybe I could help Photoshop a better one for you? Will PM. Made a new picture in collaboration with Bluedanoob! Glad I could help!

As far as the article, I like the end interview a lot. (accidentally just to that first.. my bad..) That means I skipped over the original description of the subject and when I did read the subject's description, I was a little, let down. Guy in suit seems, kind of bland in a sort of men in black government sort of way. IDK, upvoted, but I feel it could be stronger. *_*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, you can see who voted! Isn't modern science wonderful?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Wasn't expecting that" good, or "Wasn't expecting that" bad?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well.

Wasn't expecting that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't it be "date of birth: today" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. Interesting, and not too badly written, but it isn't really my capsule of cyanide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a fast write. Hope the picture doesn't look too much like something I whipped up in Paint. Enjoy!
Edit: I would like to thank toadking07 to the ends of the Earth and back for his spectacular image revamp. Hope you all agree it looks better than the original. Made my day.
